meaning: adj.不能通行的,无路可通的
examples: In ancient mythology there was no impassable gulf separating the divine from the human beings.
The avenue had never been paved, and deep mud made it impassable in winter.
A piece of impassable bog with yield of liquid peat.
On Monday, many roads across north Wales remained treacherous and some impassable, particularly minor routes.
The streets of Rangoon were impassable for hours every day that I was there.
